name: SEN/ADHD Educational Payload & Python Patcher (Companion HUD - V2)

prompts.prompt:
> **Role:** Senior Systems Architect and SEN/ADHD Pedagogy Specialist.
> **Task:** Generate the ES6 JavaScript and HTML/CSS structure for a new Web Component (`<enterprise-wagoll-task>`). Do NOT generate the Python backend script yet.
> 
> **Data Architecture (Headless Manifesto):**
> We are using a centralized data registry. The component will ONLY receive a `task-id` attribute. In its `connectedCallback()`, it must fetch its content from `window.WAGOLL_MANIFEST[this.getAttribute('task-id')]`. (Assume this global object exists).
> 
> **The 4-Phase SEN/ADHD State Machine:**
> 
> *   **Phase 0: The Prerequisite Gate (Constructive Forging)**
>     *   *UI:* Dark-slate container (`#0f172a`). Displays `manifest.cfu.question`. Render `manifest.cfu.options` as buttons.
>     *   *Behavior (Success):* If clicked option === `manifest.cfu.answer`, turn button Emerald (`#10b981`), play `window.playBeep()`, and unlock Phase 1.
>     *   *Behavior (Failure & Cooldown):* Pulse button Amber (`#f59e0b`). Disable all inputs for 3000ms. Look up the wrong answer in `manifest.cfu.misconceptions` JSON; display the specific hint.
>     *   *Behavior (Kinesthetic Drill):* On the 2nd failure, reveal a `<textarea>` styled like Mu IDE. The student MUST type the exact string found in `manifest.cfu.microDrillSyntax`. Only when `textarea.value` matches does the "Retry" button unlock. Provide a clean `removeEventListener` in `disconnectedCallback()` to prevent memory leaks.
> 
> *   **Phase 1: WAGOLL Analysis (Rubber Duck Debugging)**
>     *   *UI:* Side-by-side Flexbox. Left: `manifest.wagoll.code`. Right: `manifest.wagoll.output`. 
>     *   *Behavior:* Render a "▶ Step Over" button. Clicking it highlights the code line-by-line, displaying the corresponding explanation from `manifest.wagoll.rubberDuckArray`. Once complete, reveal Phase 2.
> 
> *   **Phase 2: IDE Synthesis Execution ("Mu-Only" Workflow)**
>     *   *UI:* Light-themed box (`#fffbeb`). Displays `manifest.mu.instructions`. Use JS to replace words like "Check" and "Debug" with CSS badges matching the Mu UI.
>     *   *Behavior:* Render a padlock input requesting `manifest.mu.verificationCode`. If correct, unlock Phase 3.
> 
> *   **Phase 3: Portfolio Reflection**
>     *   *UI:* White card. Parse `manifest.portfolio.clozeText` (which contains `[correct/wrong1/wrong2]` syntax) into `<select>` dropdowns. 
>     *   *Behavior:* On 100% correct, reveal "📸 Generate Mission Report". `onclick`, use `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` to copy the data. **CRITICAL:** Include a `.catch()` block. If clipboard fails, render a visible, auto-selected `<textarea>` instructing the student to press Ctrl+C.
> 
> Output the complete, production-ready ES6 Class enclosed in an IIFE. Use strict DRY principles and Tailwind CSS classes. No Red colors for errors, only Amber.

> **Role:** Senior Backend Python Developer. > **Task:** Generate the core engine of our Python patching script. Do NOT include the curriculum data payloads or DOM replacement logic yet. > > **Strict Technical Specifications:** > 1. **GUI Integration:** Use `tkinter` with `filedialog.askopenfilename()` and `asksaveasfilename()`. > 2. **DOM Parser (Critical):** You MUST use `BeautifulSoup` from `bs4` for all HTML manipulation. Do NOT use Regex (`re.sub`) to slice HTML tags, as it will swallow closing `` tags. Add a comment at the top: `# Requires: pip install beautifulsoup4`. > 3. **The DOM Mutator Class:** Implement a `DomPatcher(soup_object)` class. It will contain methods to find elements by ID, `clear()` their contents safely, and `append()` new elements. > 4. **Exact Byte-Delta Logging:** Use `logging` (`level=logging.DEBUG`). Calculate size via `len(html_string.encode('utf-8'))`. > * *Log Format:* `[DEBUG] {location_desc} altered. Original: {orig_size}b | New: {new_size}b | Delta: {net_change:+d}b.` > 5. **Payload Constant:** Define a constant `WEB_COMPONENT_JS_CSS = """..."""` at the top of the file to securely hold the payload generated in Prompt 1. Write a method to inject this constant into the `` of the parsed BeautifulSoup object. > > Output the Python code containing the imports, logging configuration, `DomPatcher` class, and the `run_application()` execution loop.

> **Role:** Senior Backend Python Developer.
> **Task:** We need to populate the `WAGOLL_MANIFEST` dictionary without hallucinating or losing original data. The original HTML contains legacy `<script>` tags defining `window.taskHints`, `window.SandboxRegistry`, and `window.SnippetBank`.
> 
> **Your Task:**
> Add a method `extract_legacy_data_to_manifest(self)` to the `DomPatcher` class.
> 1.  Use BeautifulSoup to find all `<script>` tags.
> 2.  Use Regex *only inside the script tag contents* to parse out the dictionaries for `taskHints`, `SandboxRegistry` (specifically extracting the `onFail` strings), and `SnippetBank`.
> 3.  Programmatically construct a new Python dictionary called `WAGOLL_MANIFEST`. For the 34 tasks (e.g., `w4-l1-p1-yd`, `w3-list`, `w8-room`), map the extracted hints and snippets into the new schema:
>     `{"cfu": {"hint": extracted_onFail_or_hint}, "wagoll": {"code": extracted_snippet}}`
> 4.  Where legacy data is missing (like `cfu.options` or `portfolio.clozeText`), provide generic B1 CEFR fallback strings (e.g., `["True", "False", "Error"]` or `"I used the [for loop / while loop] to solve this."`).
> 5.  Inject the finalized `WAGOLL_MANIFEST` as a JSON string into a new `<script>` tag in the `<head>`. Use `html.escape()` or strict JSON encoding to ensure quotes don't break the HTML parser.
> 
> Output ONLY the updated Python class containing this extraction and injection logic.

> **Role:** Senior Backend Python Developer.
> **Task:** Add `patch_curriculum_ui(self)` to the `DomPatcher` class. This method will use BeautifulSoup to safely strip the old HTML and inject `<enterprise-wagoll-task task-id="[ID]"></enterprise-wagoll-task>`.
> 
> **Category A Targets (Boss Panels):**
> *Action:* `soup.find('div', id='w4l1-p1-youdo-panel')`. Find its inner `<div class="ppt-slide-header">`. Keep the header. Call `.decompose()` on all other sibling elements inside the parent div. `.append()` the new component.
> *Target IDs:* `w4l1-p1-youdo-panel`, `w4l7-p2-youdo-panel`, `w4l8-p1-youdo-panel`, `w4l8-p2-youdo-panel`, `w5l10-p1-wedo-panel`, `w6l12-p1-youdo-panel`, `w6l12-p2-youdo-panel`, `w9-boss-youdo-panel`.
> 
> **Category B Targets (Raw Textareas):**
> *Action:* `soup.find('textarea', id='editor-w3-list')`. Navigate to its parent `.interactive-widget-box`. Call `.decompose()` on the parent. Insert component in its place.
> *Target IDs:* `editor-w3-list`, `editor-w3-juke`, `editor-w6-proj`, `editor-w7-l1-test`, `editor-w7-peer`, `editor-w7-l2-p1`, `editor-w7-l2-p2`, `editor-w8-room`, `editor-w8-boss`, `editor-w9-log`.
> 
> **Category C (Replace Legacy Tags):**
> *Action:* Find all `<wagoll-mu-task>` tags. Replace with `<enterprise-wagoll-task>`.
> 
> **Category D (Multi-Tab Projects - Lesson 10):**
> *Action:* Find the `<div class="interactive-widget-box">` containing `.w5-proj-tab` buttons. `.decompose()` it entirely. Replace sequentially with 3 tags (`proj-planets`, `proj-pwd`, `proj-isbn`).
> 
> **Category E (Extension Tasks):**
> *Action:* Find `.panel-extension` divs containing `editor-[ID]` textareas. `.decompose()` the textarea wrapper and run buttons. Append component.
> *Target IDs:* `editor-w1-ext-bidmas`, `editor-w2-ext-dracula`, `editor-w2-dice-final`, `editor-w4-ext-nest`, `editor-w5-ext-total`, `editor-w5-ext-shuffle`, `editor-w5-ext-while`, `editor-w5-ext-isbn13`, `editor-w6-ext-rgb`, `editor-w2-ext-dice`.
> 
> Output the Python code for this method. Ensure `BeautifulSoup` is used strictly to prevent swallowing adjacent HTML.

> **Role:** Senior QA Automation Engineer and Systems Architect.
> **Task:** Conduct a localized 'Fuzzy Test' on the generated code and assemble the FINAL production-ready Python script.
> 
> **Simulate the following Chaos Vectors & Edge Cases:**
> 1. **BeautifulSoup `html.parser` vs `html5lib`:** Does the script specify the parser? Defaulting to `html.parser` can sometimes strip closing tags on poorly formatted incoming documents. *Resolution:* Force `BeautifulSoup(content, 'html5lib')` or `'lxml'` and include them in the pip install comment.
> 2. **Shadow DOM Query Nulls:** In the Web Component, does `this.shadowRoot.getElementById()` fail if the element hasn't been rendered yet by the state machine? *Resolution:* Ensure safe optional chaining (`?.`) or exist checks before applying styles/classes.
> 3. **The Data Registry Race Condition:** If the Web Component initializes before `window.WAGOLL_MANIFEST` is fully parsed by the browser, it will crash. *Resolution:* Ensure the Web Component's `connectedCallback` checks if `window.WAGOLL_MANIFEST` exists, and if not, sets a brief `setTimeout` polling loop to wait for it before rendering.
> 
> **Your Output:**
> 1. Briefly document the resolution of these 3 vulnerabilities.
> 2. Produce the COMPLETE, finalized Python script. Combine imports, `BeautifulSoup` logic, `DomPatcher`, Data Extraction, DOM Mutilation, the Web Component JS/CSS constant, and the Tkinter execution loop. Do NOT use placeholders.

name: Autonomous Secondary & Post-16 Scheme of Work Generator (Y7-Y13 Complete)

prompts.prompt:
> **Phase 0: Global Initialization & Criteria Locking**
>
> You are an autonomous Curriculum Generation AI executing a continuous pipeline. You have been provided with multiple CSV delivery plans, an HTML SEND/ADHD Curriculum Plan, and statutory UK PDFs (National Curriculum, OCR Specifications).
>
> **Your Task:** Ingest these files. Acknowledge the following strict Quality Assurance (QA) criteria that you will use to evaluate and generate all subsequent work:
> 1. **Dynamic Structural Integrity:** The output MUST be a Markdown table generated **week-by-week**. You must read the CSV to determine how many lessons are scheduled for a specific week. The table columns MUST dynamically match the number of active lessons for that week (e.g., if a week has 2 lessons, the table has 2 lesson columns; if 5 lessons, 5 columns).
> 2. **Rows Required:** The rows MUST exactly match the following 9 fields: Learning Objectives, Substantive Knowledge: Conceptual, Substantive Knowledge: Procedural, Disciplinary Knowledge: Conceptual, Disciplinary Knowledge: Procedural, Declarative Knowledge, Statutory Ref / Specification Point, Common Misconceptions, Exemplar Exit Ticket.
> 3. **Statutory Veracity:** Quotes in the 'Statutory Ref' row MUST be verbatim from the UK PDFs. No paraphrasing.
> 4. **Pedagogical Depth:** No brevity bias. You must unpack concepts rigorously. 
> 5. **SEND/ADHD Alignment:** Misconceptions and Exit Tickets must directly address working-memory, impulsivity, and RSD-safe practices as per the HTML file.
> 6. **Strict UK Terminology:** All outputs MUST use British English spelling (e.g., analyse, colour) and UK educational terminology (e.g., SEND, Key Stage, Year Group, Maths). Do NOT use Americanisms like 'K-12', 'Grade 8', or 'Math'.
>
> Output ONLY: "SYSTEM INITIALIZED: All files ingested. Dynamic weekly generation and UK QA Criteria locked. Commencing autonomous generation pipeline."

> **Phase 1A: GENERATE - Year 7 (Online Safety & Spreadsheets)**

> 1. Parse `YEAR 7.csv` and the `HTML Curriculum Plan` for the 'Online Safety' and 'Spreadsheets' units.
> 2. Map the lessons to the KS3 PDF statutory quotes.
> 3. Generate the 'Scheme of Work' Markdown tables for these units **week-by-week**. Check the CSV to see how many lessons are populated for each week and generate the table columns accordingly.

> **Constraints:** Apply visual anchoring (using **bold** tags for key terminology). Ensure strict British English. Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 1B: VALIDATE & REFINE - Year 7 (Online Safety & Spreadsheets)**

> - [ ] Are all 9 rows present and correctly labelled?
> - [ ] Is the 'Statutory Ref' a direct, verbatim quote?
> - [ ] Are the Misconceptions and Exit Tickets explicitly adapted for SEND/ADHD?
> - [ ] Is the text free of Americanisms, using strictly UK terminology and spelling?
> - [ ] Did brevity bias occur?
>
> **Action:** If ANY check fails, you must completely rewrite the table to fix the errors. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables, preceded by the tag `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 2A: GENERATE - Year 7 (Programming in Scratch 1 & 2)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `YEAR 7.csv` for the 'Programming Essentials in Scratch 1' and 'Scratch 2' units.
> 2. Map to the KS3 PDF statutory quotes.
> 3. Generate the 'Scheme of Work' Markdown tables week-by-week based on the CSV lesson counts.
> 
> Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 2B: VALIDATE & REFINE - Year 7 (Programming in Scratch 1 & 2)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor.
> **Task:** Evaluate the tables generated in Phase 2A.
> 
> **Action:** Verify dynamic column count matches the CSV. Check that SEND/ADHD misconceptions focus heavily on logic-block sequencing and variable permanence. Ensure Disciplinary Knowledge defines *why* sequencing matters. Verify UK spelling. Rewrite if flawed. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables, preceded by the tag `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 3A: GENERATE - Year 7 (Networks)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `YEAR 7.csv` for the 'Networks' unit.
> 2. Map to the KS3 PDF statutory quotes regarding hardware and the internet.
> 3. Generate the 'Scheme of Work' Markdown tables week-by-week based on the CSV lesson counts.
> 
> Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 3B: VALIDATE & REFINE - Year 7 (Networks)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor.
> **Task:** Evaluate the tables from Phase 3A against the criteria.
> 
> **Action:** Verify dynamic column count. Fix any errors, particularly ensuring no brevity bias in the Substantive Conceptual row. Check for strict UK English. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables, preceded by the tag `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 4A: GENERATE - Year 8 (Online Safety & HTML)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `YEAR 8.csv` for the 'Online Safety' and 'Developing for the Web (HTML)' units.

name: Autonomous Secondary & Post-16 Science Scheme of Work Generator (Y7-Y13 Complete)

prompts.prompt:
> **Phase 0: Global Initialization & Criteria Locking (Science Curriculum Engine)**
>
> You are an autonomous Curriculum Generation AI executing a continuous, multi-phase pipeline for the Science Department (Biology, Chemistry, and Physics). You have been provided with extensive KS3 delivery plans, KS4 AQA GCSE structure matrices (B1–B7, C1–C10, P1–P8), Required Practical (RP) manifests, and statutory UK science curriculum frameworks.
>
> **Your Task:** Ingest all attached science data manifests. Acknowledge and lock the following strict Quality Assurance (QA) criteria that will govern the generation of all subsequent Schemes of Work across Key Stage 3, Key Stage 4 (AQA GCSE), and Key Stage 5 (A-Level):
> 1. **Dynamic Weekly Structural Integrity:** The output MUST be a Markdown table generated strictly **week-by-week**. You must inspect the weekly lesson allocation in the source delivery plans to determine the exact column count per week (e.g., if a week has 3 science lessons scheduled, the table must dynamically generate exactly 3 lesson columns; if 4 lessons, 4 columns).
> 2. **Mandatory 9-Row Pedagogical Matrix:** Every generated weekly table MUST contain exactly these 9 rows in strict order:
>    *   **Learning Objectives**
>    *   **Substantive Knowledge: Conceptual**
>    *   **Substantive Knowledge: Procedural**
>    *   **Disciplinary Knowledge: Conceptual (Working Scientifically)**
>    *   **Disciplinary Knowledge: Procedural (Practical / Lab Skills)**
>    *   **Declarative Knowledge (Key Vocabulary & Morphology)**
>    *   **Statutory Ref / Specification Point**
>    *   **Common Misconceptions (Diagnostic Catching)**
>    *   **Exemplar Exit Ticket (CFU & RSD-Safe Remediation)**
> 3. **Statutory Veracity & Verbatim Mappings:** All statutory quotes, AQA specification codes (e.g., `4.1.1.2`, `4.4.2.1`), National Curriculum KS3 links, and Required Practical labels (e.g., `RP1 Microscopy`, `RP5 Rates of Reaction`) MUST be extracted verbatim from the provided manifests without truncation or paraphrasing.
> 4. **SEND/ADHD Visual Scaffolding & Cognitive Rigor:** Maintain absolute academic rigor without dumbing down concepts. Resolve cognitive load via chunking: strictly adhere to a maximum 40-word limit per visible UI/table cell state. Execute visual anchoring by wrapping critical domain terminology in **bold (`**term**`)** tags.
> 5. **Strict UK Terminology:** All outputs MUST use British English spelling (e.g., *colour, analyse, neutralisation, behaviour*) and standard UK educational terminology (*Key Stage, Year Group, GCSE, Required Practical, A-Level*). Do NOT use Americanisms (*K-12, Grade 8, Math, Lab period*).
>
> Output ONLY: "SYSTEM INITIALIZED: All KS3, KS4 AQA GCSE, and KS5 Science curriculum manifests ingested. Dynamic weekly generation, 9-row pedagogical structure, and UK QA Criteria locked. Commencing autonomous generation pipeline."

> **Phase 1A: GENERATE - KS3 Year 7 Biology Core (Safety, Lab Skills & Microscopy)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `KS3 Structure_Lab skills.csv` and `KS3 Structure_Cells.csv` (Lessons 1 to 7).
> 2. Map lessons across: **Safety in the Laboratory**, **Hazard Symbols**, **Using Laboratory Equipment**, **Bunsen Burners**, and **Cell Structure & Microscopy**.
> 3. Generate the week-by-week Scheme of Work Markdown tables. Inspect the source schedule to dynamically align the column count to active weekly lesson allocations.
> 
> **Constraints:** Integrate Working Scientifically (WS) procedural skills directly into the *Disciplinary Knowledge* rows. Explicitly document diagnostic misconceptions (e.g., mixing up coarse/fine focus wheels, or assuming hazards and risks are identical). Apply strict visual anchoring (**bold terms**). Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 1B: VALIDATE & REFINE - KS3 Year 7 Biology Core (Safety, Lab Skills & Microscopy)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor & Science Pedagogy Specialist.
> **Task:** Evaluate the tables generated in Phase 1A against the Global QA Criteria.
> 
> **Execute the following internal audit checks:**
> - [ ] Does the lesson column count dynamically match the exact weekly lesson schedule?
> - [ ] Are all 9 pedagogical rows present, correctly ordered, and unabridged?
> - [ ] Are statutory National Curriculum KS3 links quoted verbatim?
> - [ ] Do the *Common Misconceptions* and *Exemplar Exit Tickets* directly address working-memory and RSD-safe remediation (e.g., constructive cues instead of red X's)?
> - [ ] Is visual anchoring (`**bold**`) applied to all substantive vocabulary?
> - [ ] Is the text free of Americanisms and brevity bias?
>
> **Action:** If ANY check fails, completely rewrite the affected tables. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables, preceded by the exact tag `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 2A: GENERATE - KS3 Year 7 Biology & Chemistry Core (Movement of Substances & Reproduction)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `KS3 Structure_Cells.csv` (Lessons 8 to 10) and `KS3 Structure_Reproduction.csv`.
> 2. Map lessons across: **Diffusion & Osmosis**, **Unicellular Organisms**, **Adolescence & Puberty**, **Human Reproductive Systems**, **Fertilisation, Pregnancy & Birth**, and **Plant Reproduction/Seed Dispersal**.
> 3. Generate the week-by-week Scheme of Work Markdown tables adhering strictly to dynamic column sizing.
> 
> **Constraints:** Ensure particle explanations of diffusion and anatomical structure breakdowns adhere to the 40-word cell chunking rule. Document misconceptions (e.g., confusing adolescence with puberty or equating sperm with semen). Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 2B: VALIDATE & REFINE - KS3 Year 7 Biology & Chemistry Core (Movement of Substances & Reproduction)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor & Science Pedagogy Specialist.
> **Task:** Evaluate the tables generated in Phase 2A.
> 
> **Action:** Verify dynamic column sizing against the CSV schedule. Confirm that *Disciplinary Knowledge: Procedural* details specific practical methods (e.g., agar diffusion or flower dissection). Ensure strict British English and visual anchoring. Rewrite if flawed. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables with `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 3A: GENERATE - KS3 Year 7 Chemistry Core (Particle Model & States of Matter)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `KS3 Structure_Particles.csv`.
> 2. Map lessons across: **The Particle Model**, **Melting & Freezing**, **Cooling Curves (Stearic Acid)**, **Boiling vs Evaporation**, **Pure & Impure Substances**, and **Gas Pressure**.
> 3. Generate the week-by-week Scheme of Work Markdown tables with dynamic column matching.
> 
> **Constraints:** Ensure heating and cooling curves are thoroughly scaffolded in *Substantive Knowledge: Procedural*. Embed diagnostic misconception catching regarding particles expanding when heated or bubbles containing air during boiling. Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 3B: VALIDATE & REFINE - KS3 Year 7 Chemistry Core (Particle Model & States of Matter)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor & Science Pedagogy Specialist.
> **Task:** Evaluate Phase 3A.
> 
> **Action:** Audit the 9-row structure. Verify that gas pressure relationships ($P \propto 1/V$ and temperature impacts) are accurately represented in *Disciplinary Knowledge: Conceptual*. Confirm SEND/ADHD accommodations prevent text bloat across complex tables. Rewrite if flawed. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables with `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 4A: GENERATE - KS3 Year 7 Chemistry & Physics Core (Elements, Atoms & Compounds)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `KS3 Structure_Atoms, Elements, compound & mix.csv`.
> 2. Map lessons across: **Elements & The Periodic Table**, **Atomic Structure**, **Compounds vs Mixtures**, **Chemical Formulae**, **Solutions & Solubility**, and **Separation Techniques (Filtration, Evaporation, Distillation, Chromatography)**.
> 3. Generate the week-by-week Scheme of Work Markdown tables.
> 
> **Constraints:** Ensure chemical symbols, compound naming rules (`-ide` vs `-ate`), and Rf calculations are clearly delineated. Address ADHD cognitive load by segmenting multi-step separation practicals. Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 4B: VALIDATE & REFINE - KS3 Year 7 Chemistry & Physics Core (Elements, Atoms & Compounds)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor & Science Pedagogy Specialist.
> **Task:** Evaluate Phase 4A.
> 
> **Action:** Verify dynamic column sizing. Check that common misconceptions (e.g., confusing molecules of elements with molecules of compounds, or dipping chromatography start lines in solvent) are fully diagnosed. Ensure UK terminology. Rewrite if flawed. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables with `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 5A: GENERATE - KS3 Year 7 Physics Core (Forces & Space Physics)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `KS3 Structure_Forces.csv` and `KS3 Structure_Space.csv`.
> 2. Map lessons across: **Contact & Non-Contact Forces**, **Resultant Forces & Equilibrium**, **Hooke's Law & Elasticity**, **Drag & Friction**, **Mass vs Weight ($W = mg$)**, **History of the Universe**, **The Solar System**, **Gravity & Orbits**, **The Moon & Eclipses**, and **Day, Night & Seasons**.
> 3. Generate the week-by-week Scheme of Work Markdown tables.
> 
> **Constraints:** Unpack force arrows, vector magnitudes, and astronomical scales with explicit procedural steps. Address classical misconceptions (e.g., equating mass with weight or believing summer occurs because Earth is closer to the Sun). Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 5B: VALIDATE & REFINE - KS3 Year 7 Physics Core (Forces & Space Physics)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor & Science Pedagogy Specialist.
> **Task:** Evaluate Phase 5A.
> 
> **Action:** Check 100% compliance with the 9-row structure. Verify that spring extension calculations and solar/lunar eclipse ray diagrams are explicitly detailed without brevity bias. Confirm `[QA_PASSED]` criteria. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables.

> **Phase 6A: GENERATE - KS3 Year 8 Biology Core (Health, Nutrition & Ecosystems)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `KS3 Structure_Health and Nutrition.csv` and `KS3 Structure_Ecosystems.csv`.
> 2. Map lessons across: **Nutrients & Balanced Diets**, **Food Tests Practical (Starch, Lipids, Sugars, Proteins)**, **Deficiency Diseases & Energy Requirements**, **Drugs, Alcohol & Smoking**, **Cellular Respiration (Aerobic vs Anaerobic & Fermentation)**, **Photosynthesis & Limiting Factors**, **Leaf Structure & Stomata**, and **Food Chains, Webs & Ecosystem Bioaccumulation**.
> 3. Generate the week-by-week Scheme of Work Markdown tables.
> 
> **Constraints:** Ensure word equations for respiration and photosynthesis are explicitly detailed. Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 6B: VALIDATE & REFINE - KS3 Year 8 Biology Core (Health, Nutrition & Ecosystems)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor & Science Pedagogy Specialist.
> **Task:** Evaluate Phase 6A.
> 
> **Action:** Audit dynamic columns and exact curriculum mapping. Ensure food test procedures detail reagent colors and heating requirements within *Disciplinary Knowledge: Procedural*. Confirm RSD-safe Exit Tickets. Rewrite if flawed. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables with `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 7A: GENERATE - KS3 Year 8 Chemistry Core (Acids, Alkalis & Chemical Reactions)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `KS3 Structure_Acid and Alkalis.csv` and `KS3 Structure_Chemical Reactions.csv`.
> 2. Map lessons across: **Properties of Acids & Alkalis**, **pH Scale & Indicators**, **Neutralisation & Making Salts**, **Chemical vs Physical Changes**, **Word & Symbol Equations**, **Combustion & Thermal Decomposition**, **Conservation of Mass**, **Exothermic & Endothermic Reactions**, and **Reactivity of Metals (Acids, Oxygen, Water & Displacement)**.
> 3. Generate the week-by-week Scheme of Work Markdown tables.
> 
> **Constraints:** Provide comprehensive scaffolding for MASH ($Metal + Acid \rightarrow Salt + Hydrogen$) equations and salt crystal preparations. Output the drafted tables.

> **Phase 7B: VALIDATE & REFINE - KS3 Year 8 Chemistry Core (Acids, Alkalis & Chemical Reactions)**
>
> **Role:** Strict QA Automation Auditor & Science Pedagogy Specialist.
> **Task:** Evaluate Phase 7A.
> 
> **Action:** Check chemistry tables for cognitive load limits (max 40 words per visible step). Verify exact National Curriculum links. Confirm that common errors (e.g., assuming mass is lost when gas escapes) are addressed. Rewrite if flawed. Output ONLY the FINAL, validated Markdown tables with `[QA_PASSED]`.

> **Phase 8A: GENERATE - KS3 Year 8 Physics Core (Energy & Electricity/Magnetism)**
>
> **Task:**
> 1. Parse `KS3 Structure_Energy.csv` and `KS3 Structure_Electricity & Magnetism.csv`.
> 2. Map lessons across: **Energy Stores & Transfers**, **Conservation & Efficiency ($E_{out}/E_{in}$)**, **Internal Energy & Heat Transfers (Conduction, Convection, Radiation)**, **Work Done ($W=Fd$) & Power ($P=E/t$)**, **Energy Resources & Domestic Bills**, **Static Charge & Electric Fields**, **Circuits, Current & Voltage**, **Series vs Parallel Rules**, **Resistance ($V=IR$)**, and **Electromagnets & Motors**.
